meta_title: For Students: idea validation to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Idea Validation to Solve Debugging Paralysis
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn how to validate your AI ideas before building to ensure technical viability using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Debugging paralysis often stems from building unvalidated, overly complex architectures. By applying rigorous idea validation, you prune non-essential features, drastically reducing your codebase's surface area. Use Antigravity to simulate architectural flows and Claude Code to automate refactoring, ensuring every line of code serves a clear, validated product objective.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal overflowing with red stack traces, the cursor blinking like a heartbeat in a morgue. You’ve been building for three days, and the logic has folded in on itself—a labyrinth of your own making. This isn't a failure of intelligence; it’s a failure of scope. You are trying to debug a product that hasn't been validated against reality.

True senior engineers don't just write code; they validate the problem space before the first commit. By shifting your focus from 'making it work' to 'building what matters,' you transform from a frustrated student into a strategic architect. We’re going to use Antigravity and Claude Code to strip away the noise and rebuild your project on a foundation of validated logic.

steps.title: Define the Minimal Viable Logic
steps.description: Before debugging, isolate the core value proposition. If the logic is too complex to track, it's too complex to ship. We strip the project back to its essential state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project architecture. Identify the top 20% of code paths that deliver 80% of the user value. Suggest a simplified data flow that removes secondary features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'refactor to remove non-essential modules identified in the architectural audit'

steps.title: Automated Architectural Simulation
steps.description: Use Antigravity to simulate how data moves through your system. This prevents logical dead-ends before they manifest as runtime errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run a stress-test simulation on the proposed data flow. Identify potential race conditions or state management conflicts that would lead to debugging bottlenecks.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Points
steps.description: Use Claude Code to surgically extract the problematic modules. By isolating code, you gain the clarity needed to resolve complex dependencies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a visual map of the current error propagation in the codebase. Where is the logic breaking down under load?
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --path ./src/modules/error-prone-zone --command 'isolate and unit test these functions'

steps.title: Iterative Refactoring with Context
steps.description: Apply fixes based on the validated architecture. Claude Code maintains the context, preventing the 'fix one bug, create two more' cycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a refactoring strategy for the isolated modules that adheres to clean code principles and ensures modularity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude fix --apply --message 'implementing validated refactor based on architectural audit'

steps.title: Validation Loop Integration
steps.description: Embed a validation check at every commit. This ensures that future code additions align with the validated project goals.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a checklist for future commits that validates new code against the current project architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'setup pre-commit validation hook for architectural consistency'

pro_tips: Treat your codebase like a startup: pivot the code if the architecture isn't scaling.
If a feature takes more than 4 hours to debug, it likely shouldn't exist in your MVP.
Use Claude Code to write your documentation as you code; it keeps the logic and the explanation in sync.
Antigravity simulations are more reliable than manual testing for edge-case identification.
Always commit after every successful validation milestone; it creates a 'safe zone' for experimentation.
content.next_steps: Once your core logic is validated and the bugs are squashed, deploy your MVP to a staging environment and run a user-acceptance test to confirm your validation hypothesis.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the methodology to stop spinning your wheels and start shipping production-grade applications. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
